revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|qq=draft|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Dekanstern (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Seba-en-Sah]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Seba-en-Sah]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]]

''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|qq=draft|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Seba-en-Sah]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] }} [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]]

Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. revision.comment: Bot: tag pages missing wikidata links
revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|qq=draft|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Seba-en-Sah]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] }} [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[sn:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|qq=draft|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|qq=draft|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2262463}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[de:Seba-en-Sah]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2262463}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2262463}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the [[:en:Sothic cycle|Sothic cycle]]. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was [[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]], excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the [[:en:Midnight|midnight]] [[:en:Culmination|culmination]] of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November [[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the [[:en:Planet|planet]] [[:en:Mars|Mars]], even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Egyptian calendar|Egyptian calendar]] !style="background:#FFE39B"|[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut}}’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en|Sothic cycle|WD=Q1749234|lt=Sothic cycle}}. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was {{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|WD=Q284009|lt=Elephantine}}, excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the {{ill|Midnight|en|Midnight|WD=Q36402|lt=midnight}} {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=culmination}} of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November {{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planet}} {{ill|Mars|en|Mars|WD=Q111|lt=Mars}}, even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Egyptian calendar}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] {{wikidata link|Q2262463}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planet}} {{ill|Mars|en|Mars|WD=Q111|lt=Mars}}, even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Egyptian calendar}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME = S29-Aa17-V28-D61-N14:N35
Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut|1=Senenmut}}’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en|Sothic cycle|WD=Q1749234|lt=Sothic cycle|1=Sothic cycle}}. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was {{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|WD=Q284009|lt=Elephantine|1=Elephantine}}, excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the {{ill|Midnight|en|Midnight|WD=Q36402|lt=midnight|1=Midnight}} {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=culmination|1=Culmination}} of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November {{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planet|1=Planet}} {{ill|Mars|en|Mars|WD=Q111|lt=Mars|1=Mars}}, even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Egyptian calendar|1=Egyptian calendar}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 === References === {{translated page|de|Seba-en-Sah|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Seba-en-Sah]] {{wikidata link|Q2262463}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]
