Seba-en-Sah
''Sb3-n-S3ḥ''
''Star of the Descending One (Toe or Foot Star)'' |BILD1 = Orion-Sternkarte.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation "Sah" (Orion) }} '''Seba-en-Sah''' (also '''Tepi-a Sah'''; ''First of the Descending One'') is the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} name for {{ill|Rigel|de|Rigel (Stern)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rigel|qq=}} ({{ill|Beta Orionis|de|β Orionis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beta Orionis|qq=}}), the principal star of the {{ill|Sah (constellation)|de|Sah (Sternbild)|lt=Sah|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sah (constellation)|qq=}} constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy. == Mythological Connections == Seba-en-Sah served as the namesake for the Sah constellation and as a dekan star depicted on the astronomical ceiling of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut|1=Senenmut}}’s tomb and in the {{ill|day selection calendar|de|Tagewählkalender (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day selection calendar|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=}}, Seba-en-Sah represented the beginning of the {{ill|Fifth Hour of the Night|de|Fünfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Hour of the Night|qq=}} on {{ill|1 Achet IV|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet IV|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 135. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss (Ägyptologe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} rejected the identification of Seba-en-Sah with Rigel based on his findings about the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en|Sothic cycle|WD=Q1749234|lt=Sothic cycle|1=Sothic cycle}}. He argued that the initial reference point for the heliacal rise of Sirius was {{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|WD=Q284009|lt=Elephantine|1=Elephantine}}, excluding the possibility of identifying Seba-en-Sah with Rigel: {{Zitat|Krauss must assert this,… otherwise his entire calculations would collapse… The means involve a distorted and substantially incomplete representation of alternative arguments.|Christian Leitz, ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''}} [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|The astronomical ceiling in the tomb of Senenmut]] In the Ramesside star clocks, the {{ill|Midnight|en|Midnight|WD=Q36402|lt=midnight|1=Midnight}} {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=culmination|1=Culmination}} of Seba-en-Sah is recorded for {{ill|23 and 24 Achet III|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/23 and 24 Achet III|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}}.14 and 15 November {{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} in 1463 BCE. This culmination occurred during the middle of the {{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}}. Remarkably, this date aligns precisely with the details found on the astronomical ceiling of the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=}} (Senenmut’s tomb), suggesting the astronomical arrangements were drawn up in 1463 BCE. It is therefore no coincidence that the astronomical ceiling in Senenmut’s tomb served as a template for dekan arrangements, which continued to be copied during the Ramesside period. Another piece of evidence supporting the copying of such ceilings in later periods is the absence of the {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planet|1=Planet}} {{ill|Mars|en|Mars|WD=Q111|lt=Mars|1=Mars}}, even though Mars appears in other texts.Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. p. 136. == Hourly Assignments == {| class="wikitable center" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="3" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Start of Ancient Egyptian Night Hours for Seba-en-Sah in 1463 BCE''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|Night Hour !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Egyptian calendar|1=Egyptian calendar}} !style="background:#FFE39B"|{{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|lt=Twelfth Hour of the Night|de|Zwölfte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth hour of the night (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} |{{ill|1 Achet I|de|Achet#Datierungen|1=Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|lt=Achet|lt=1 Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1 Achet I|13=User:Immanuelle/Season_of_the_Inundation#Months|qq=}} |11 August |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Tenth Hour of the Night|de|Zehnte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet II |10 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |{{ill|Eighth Hour of the Night|de|Achte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Hour of the Night|qq=}} |1 Achet III |10 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |{{ill|Seventh Hour of the Night|de|Siebte Nachtstunde (Altes Ägypten)|lt=7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventh Hour of the Night|qq=}} |16 Achet III |25 October |- |} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''.
